mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in INDPRO signals expanding real output and improving operating leverage, causing higher expected earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available INDPRO was 113.1336 as of two months before 2006-11-15
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO momentum is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in 50% or fewer walk-forward observations, or Rank IC is below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 5.0%, tight policy raises discount rates and increases refinancing pressure, causing lower equity valuations and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available FEDFUNDS was 5.25 as of one month before 2006-11-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered FEDFUNDS observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if mean triggered returns are not below unconditional returns at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in HOUST signals weakening residential investment and future credit stress, reducing expected cyclical earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average after being above it
evidence_refs: fact:latest available HOUST was 1772.0 as of two months before 2006-11-15
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downside crossings are not followed by below-baseline 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if the hit rate for negative returns after triggers is below 55% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A low and falling UNRATE combined with rising PAYEMS signals resilient household income and stable demand, supporting earnings revisions and causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE below 4.8% and PAYEMS higher than three months earlier
evidence_refs: fact:latest available UNRATE was 4.4 and PAYEMS was 135844.0 as of one month before 2006-11-15
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-UNRATE and rising-PAYEMS condition does not outperform unconditional 20-day returns, or if either component alone fully explains the signal in interaction tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL during an already high FEDFUNDS regime signals persistent inflation that delays easing and lifts real-rate uncertainty, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth rises while FEDFUNDS is above 5.0%
evidence_refs: fact:latest available CPIAUCSL was 202.7 and FEDFUNDS was 5.25 before 2006-11-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered inflation-plus-tight-policy observations have nonnegative average 5-day returns, or if the interaction term between CPIAUCSL growth and FEDFUNDS is not negative in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in RSAFS signals stronger nominal consumption and near-term revenue growth, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when labor income is stable.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS above its 3-month moving average and PAYEMS not lower than three months earlier
evidence_refs: fact:latest available RSAFS was 363673.0 and PAYEMS was 135844.0 before 2006-11-15
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum with nondeclining PAYEMS fails to produce above-median 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the joint signal underperforms an RSAFS-only signal.

mechanism_narrative: Slowing GDPC1 growth after an expansion peak signals decelerating aggregate demand and weaker earnings momentum, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 year-over-year growth crosses below its trailing 4-quarter average
evidence_refs: fact:latest available GDPC1 was 11432.9 as of four months before 2006-11-15
failure_condition: Reject if GDPC1 growth downside crossings are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if average triggered returns exceed unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: An upward crossing in UNRATE after a period below 4.8% signals deterioration in labor demand and rising recession risk, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months after being below 4.8%
evidence_refs: fact:latest available UNRATE was 4.4 as of one month before 2006-11-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases do not predict below-baseline 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if negative-return hit rate after triggers is less than 55% in walk-forward testing.
